title: One-Shot Bodies, Streaming, Uploads, Decompression, and Resource Limits · Automation and Data Client Systems · Failure Triage
summary: A data reliability engineer tracing a failed overnight import must isolate a plausible but incorrect result from conflicting traces, repair its cause, and retain a regression witness for one-shot bodies, streaming, uploads, decompression, and resource limits, producing bounded streaming body pipeline. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers bounded streaming body pipeline and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Lea…
objectives: Treat request and response bodies as owned one-shot streams with explicit read, close, replay, and cleanup rules. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Limit compressed and decoded response reads before allocation, parsing, logging, or persistence. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Stream large uploads with known ownership, backpressure, progress, cancellation, and retryability. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

steps.title: Python Inspect the failure record · Debug One Shot Body Ownership
steps.instruction:
debug case — overnight ingestion repair client streams a 12 MiB upload and receives a 40 KiB compressed body that expands past 30 MiB. Define one-shot ownership, replay, compressed and decoded limits, cancellation, trailers, and cleanup. Competency probe: Treat request and response bodies as owned one-shot streams with explicit read, close, replay, and cleanup rules. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
